weekly_summary: This week marks the START of a structured rebuild. You've been significantly undertraining over the past 8 weeks with only 1-3 runs per week and <10 km most weeks. We're moving from sporadic training to a consistent 5-run structure that uses your full 7-hour budget. All runs this week are Zone 2 (easy aerobic pace ~6:00-6:20 min/km). No intensity work until aerobic base is re-established. Focus: frequency and consistency over intensity. Enable HR monitor for all runs.
coaching_notes: You have a 7-hour weekly budget but have been using only 1-2 hours. This week, we reclaim that capacity through consistent easy runs. Your pace (5:47–6:18 min/km) is solid and sustainable, but at marathon goal pace, you need volume first. No heart rate data means I cannot fine-tune intensity — please get a HR monitor (Garmin, Polar, Apple Watch, or simple chest strap). Next week, if you complete all 5 runs, we'll add 1 tempo session. Target: 5-6 runs per week for next 4 weeks to build to 45-50 km/week, then introduce structured intensity.
recovery_flags: Acute:Chronic ratio 0.65 suggests recent detraining — expect soreness return if volume increases >15% this week (you're at 38 km, up from 9.9); this is acceptable for re-building
No HR data to assess recovery readiness — monitor RHR (resting heart rate) each morning if possible
If any run feels excessively hard at prescribed easy pace, skip intensity and repeat day as easy run
next_week_preview: Week 10 (June 29 - July 5): If all 5 runs completed, introduce 1 structured tempo run (20 min @ 5:50 pace with 10 min warm-up/cool-down). Increase long run to 14 km. Target 42-45 km. All other runs remain Zone 2. Continue HR monitoring.

days.title: Recovery & Consistency Restart
days.description: Easy conversational pace to re-establish training rhythm. You ran 3.62 km on this date; this plan extends it. Focus on relaxed running, steady breathing, able to speak in full sentences.
days.notes: If you already completed the 3.62 km run, do an additional 3.4 km easy in the evening, or skip and move to Tuesday.

days.title: Easy Base Run
days.description: Steady easy run at 6:00 min/km pace. This is your aerobic foundation for marathon training. All effort goes into building volume, not speed.
days.notes: Target HR (if available): estimate 65-75% of max. For a 35-year-old, that's roughly 120-150 bpm; adjust based on your fitness. Feel should be 'easy conversation pace'.

days.title: Active Recovery / Strength
days.description: Full rest day OR 45 min easy cross-training (cycling, elliptical, swimming, yoga). Strength work optional: 3x10 squats, 3x10 lunges, 3x8 single-leg hops per leg (builds running durability). No running.
days.notes: Given your undertraining, a full rest day is acceptable. Strength work prevents injury when volume increases.

days.title: Easy Base Run
days.description: You already ran 6.31 km on this date. If so, add 2.2 km easy this evening. Otherwise, run the full 8.5 km as a steady easy run.
days.notes: Pace 6:00 min/km. No pressure to run fast. This is your second run of the week if you didn't run Monday.

days.title: Easy Base Run
days.description: Steady easy run, middle of the week completion. This builds aerobic base and preps you for the weekend long run.
days.notes: Pace 6:00 min/km. Fourth running day of the week. Feel should be recovered and strong.

days.title: Weekly Long Run
days.description: Your first structured long run in this plan. Run at easy, conversational pace — exactly the pace you've been using (6:05-6:15 min/km). This is the cornerstone of marathon training. Do NOT rush. Bring water and fuel if running >90 min (e.g., sports drink, energy gel at 60 min mark).
days.description_extended: Marathon training requires teaching your body to run for 2+ hours. This 13 km run at easy pace (~78 min) is step one. Next week we'll build to 14 km, then 16, 18, 20+. Focus on nailing the pace and fueling strategy here.
days.notes: If HR available, keep at Zone 2. If you feel strong, do NOT speed up — save energy for future weeks. Eat something light 2 hrs before; refuel within 30 min after.

days.title: Full Recovery Day
days.description: Complete rest. Your body builds fitness during recovery. Sleep 8+ hours, hydrate, eat protein and carbs. Review HR data from the week (if available) and note how you felt on each run.
days.notes: This is your only full rest day this week. Treat it seriously. Light walking (15-30 min) is fine but no structured running.
